layout.flexibility: WIM UI provides a rich set of layout components from simple linear stacks to multi-dimensional grids. Choose the right one based on the structure and flexibility you need.
layout.linear_stack: Linear stacking of elements in one direction
layout.stack_usage: Vertical or horizontal list, spacing between cards
layout.flexible_flex: Flexible arrangement with fine-grained Flexbox control
layout.flex_usage: Horizontally aligned icons and text, custom gap
layout.multi_dim_grid: Multi-dimensional layout with rows and columns
layout.basic_box: Basic box wrapper with style props
layout.box_usage: Wrapping with background color or padding
layout.container_usage: Centering page content within a maximum width
layout.bento_desc: Asymmetric grid cells spanning multiple rows and columns
layout.masonry_desc: Items fill columns top-to-bottom with varying heights
layout.splitter_desc: Drag handle between panes to resize; ideal for editors and split views
layout.gap_control_desc: Control spacing with the `gap` prop on Stack, Flex, Grid, or Group rather than adding margins to children.
layout.simple_grid_responsive_desc: Use SimpleGrid's `cols` prop with breakpoint objects to create responsive column layouts without custom CSS.
layout.box_as_prop_desc: Use Box's `component` prop (e.g., `component="section"`) to render semantic HTML while applying design-system styles.
layout.container_max_width_desc: Wrap page content in Container and set `size` to a breakpoint token to ensure readable line lengths on wide screens.

guide.layout_desc: Guidelines for roles and placement of Header, Sidebar, Main, and Footer.
guide.layout_footer_content: Recommended: Copyright, terms of service, privacy policy, language switcher, social links
guide.layout_footer_desc: Contains supplementary or legal information. Often omitted in web applications but critical for websites.
guide.layout_header_content: Recommended: Logo, global search, notifications, user profile, top-level navigation
guide.layout_header_desc: Provides context for the entire application and access to global actions. Should include features accessible from anywhere.
guide.layout_main_desc: The central area where users perform tasks. Data display and operations happen here.
guide.layout_overlay_desc: Layer for catching user attention or inputting supplementary info without interrupting the workflow.
guide.layout_pageheader_content: Recommended: Page title, breadcrumbs, primary action buttons, filter toggles
guide.layout_pageheader_desc: Contains the current page title, breadcrumbs, and primary actions for that page (create, save, delete, etc.).
guide.layout_responsive_desc: Adapts the layout based on screen size. Typically, sidebars become drawers and header menus collapse into hamburger menus on mobile.
guide.layout_responsive_mobile: Mobile (< 768px): Sidebar -> Drawer, Header Menu -> Hamburger
guide.layout_rightpanel_content: Recommended: Property panels, comment threads, chat, help documentation
guide.layout_rightpanel_desc: Displays details of selected items, settings, help, or notification history. Can be permanent or temporarily triggered.
guide.layout_sidebar_content: Recommended: Main menu, project lists, filtering options, category lists
guide.layout_sidebar_desc: Manages primary navigation within the application and hierarchical movement. Suitable for drill-down menus.
